children.textZhHtml: 您不赞成增加监管，除非涉及您理解和关心的问题。对你不关心和/或不了解的一切的所有监督和监管都是政府的过度干预。每个政府机构都是对你税金的无用浪费，除了那些你依赖的机构和你有朋友在那里工作的机构。我有这个权利吗？

children.author: pseudocomposer
children.textRawHtml:
I think “when you buy a product, be it a game, a house, a car, a computer, a tractor, washer, TV, it should continue to operate without rent-seeking behavior” is the best type of straightforward, uniformly-applicable pattern of regulation one could hope for. Opposing rent-seeking is literally why we have American democracy, which paved the way for French, Brazilian, Canadian, Indian, Mexican and so many other democracies. Kings were the ultimate rent-seekers: every citizen was the product.

It’s not like this is some special case. People make the exact same arguments against John Deere, Tesla, Apple etc. And it’s a major reason many understand we should favor local (or local-capable/open-weight) AI/LLMs. I think “for any product whose support is discontinued, with more than X users, either open source all relevant software and hardware schematics, or provide a binary that will work on the hardware in perpetuity without DRM checks, based on industry” is a miniscule request in the face of any of these industries. I’d say, for instance, weights for discontinued Claude and OpenAI versions would fit. And it’s exactly the type of problem (functioning) democracies are meant for.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为“当你购买一种产品时，无论是游戏、房子、汽车、电脑、拖拉机、洗衣机、电视，它都应该继续运行，没有寻租行为”是人们所希望的最好的直接、统一适用的监管模式。反对寻租实际上就是我们拥有美国民主的原因，它为法国、巴西、加拿大、印度、墨西哥和许多其他民主国家铺平了道路。国王是终极寻租者：每个公民都是产品。

这并不是什么特殊情况。人们对约翰迪尔、特斯拉、苹果等提出了完全相同的论点。这是许多人认为我们应该支持本地（或本地能力/开放权重）人工智能/法学硕士的主要原因。我认为，“对于任何已停止支持、拥有超过 X 个用户的产品，要么开源所有相关的软件和硬件原理图，要么根据行业提供无需 DRM 检查即可在硬件上永久运行的二进制文件”，面对这些行业中的任何一个，都是一个微不足道的要求。例如，我想说，停产的 Claude 和 OpenAI 版本的重量是合适的。这正是民主国家所面临的问题类型。

children.author: JuniperMesos
children.textRawHtml:
> Opposing rent-seeking is literally why we have American democracy, which paved the way for French, Brazilian, Canadian, Indian, Mexican and so many other democracies. Kings were the ultimate rent-seekers: every citizen was the product.

It's really not. Opposing rent-seeking in a general sense isn't what motivated the American revolution; and the King of England in the 18th century wasn't primarily a rent-seeker. The royal family did and does own a great deal of land in Britain which they collect rents on, but this is true of a lot of the historic and current British nobility; and the institution of the British monarchy was and is doing a lot of other things socially that just have no relationship to rent-seeking one way or the other. Ruling monarchs aren't "rent-seekers" where their citizens are "products", except insofar as any government of any group of people is; and I think that's way too reductive a way to explain why societies and governments work the way they do.

Rent-seeking is a temptation that all sorts of people under all sorts of political and economic systems are prone to. Democracy is no particular guard against it, because people who benefit from rent-seeking in some particular set of circumstances can vote too.

This isn't to say that rent-seeking is good, but it's also a pretty hard thing to regulate. It's really hard to codify in law which economic activities are rent-seeking and which ones are people buying a product or service that someone else thinks is a bad deal for them.
children.textZhHtml:
> 反对寻租实际上就是我们拥有美国民主的原因，这为法国、巴西、加拿大、印度、墨西哥和许多其他民主国家铺平了道路。国王是终极寻租者：每个公民都是产品。

事实并非如此。一般意义上的反对寻租并不是美国革命的动机；反寻租并不是美国革命的动力。 18 世纪的英国国王主要并不是一个寻租者。王室在英国过去和现在都拥有大量土地并收取租金，但许多历史上和现在的英国贵族都是如此。英国君主制的制度过去和现在在社会上做了很多其他事情，但这些事情与寻租无关。统治君主不是“寻租者”，而他们的公民则是“产品”，除非任何群体的任何政府都是“寻租者”。我认为，用这种方式来解释社会和政府为何如此运作的方式过于简单化。

寻租是各种政治和经济制度下的各种人都容易受到的诱惑。民主并没有特别防范它，因为在某些特定情况下从寻租中受益的人也可以投票。

这并不是说寻租是好事，但它也是一件很难监管的事情。很难在法律中规定哪些经济活动是寻租，哪些是人们购买别人认为对他们来说不划算的产品或服务。

children.author: flumes_whims_
children.textRawHtml: Members of parliament who owned stock in the East India company bailing it out by allowing it to sell tea directly to the American colonies?
children.textZhHtml: 持有东印度公司股份的议员们通过允许其直接向美洲殖民地销售茶叶来为其纾困？

children.author: sdwr
children.textRawHtml: No taxation without representation?
children.textZhHtml: 无代表不纳税？

children.author: Terr_
children.textRawHtml:
Also: Those ownership/privacy obligations to customers should come first in bankruptcy proceedings, ahead of other debts.

IANABankrupcyLawyer, but I believe the status-quo is that various promises like "we'll open-source the server in the end" or "we'll never sell your data" can become voided in the name of making a buck to repay the landlord or business-partner.
children.textZhHtml:
此外：在破产程序中，对客户的所有权/隐私义务应放在首位，先于其他债务。

IANABankrupcyLawyer，但我认为现状是，诸如“我们最终将开源服务器”或“我们永远不会出售您的数据”之类的各种承诺可能会以赚钱偿还房东或业务合作伙伴的名义而变得无效。

children.author: inigyou
children.textRawHtml: Reasonable compromise: do this or your copyright is voided.
children.textZhHtml: 合理的妥协：要么这么做，要么你的版权作废。

children.author: account42
children.textRawHtml: I think the obligation should be transfer to the copyright holder in the case of bankruptcy. They can then choose to either make individual buyers whole or give up copyright protection.
children.textZhHtml: 我认为在破产情况下，这项义务应转移给版权持有者。然后他们可以选择要么赔偿个别买家使其免受损失，要么放弃版权保护。

children.author: Terr_
children.textRawHtml:
That might work for static videos and music where the thing isn't interactive, but I don't think it maps cleanly to things like games with an online component, or "we won't sell your private data" SaaS promises.

For example, imagine an MMO, where the buyer says: "I want the trademark and the copyright to all textures and 3D models, but I am not buying the copyright to the source code because I'm going to make a sequel."
children.textZhHtml:
这可能适用于非交互式的静态视频和音乐，但我认为它不能清楚地映射到带有在线组件的游戏或“我们不会出售您的私人数据”SaaS 承诺之类的东西。

例如，想象一个 MMO，买家说：“我想要所有纹理和 3D 模型的商标和版权，但我不会购买源代码的版权，因为我要制作一个续集。”

children.author: NikolaNovak
children.textRawHtml:
>>I think “when you buy a product, be it a game, a house, a car, a computer, a tractor, washer, TV, it should continue to operate without rent-seeking behavior” is the best type of straightforward, uniformly-applicable pattern of regulation one could hope for.

This is why I love Hacker News; I feel you genuinely believe this - that designing and enforcing the law around digital property rights is the easy, straightforward, and the priority/important law :).

(I don't disagree! I'm a computer geek too, this stuff is important and visible to me as well. But let's have awareness of a) the actual complications around crafting and implementing laws around something so massively complex and constantly changing, and b) where the actual priorities for vast majority of people in the world may be:)
children.textZhHtml:
>>我认为“当你购买一个产品时，无论是游戏、房子、汽车、电脑、拖拉机、洗衣机、电视，它都应该在没有任何影响的情况下继续运行”“寻租行为”是人们所希望的最好的直接、统一适用的监管模式。

这就是我喜欢 Hacker News 的原因；我觉得你真诚相信这一点 - 围绕数字产权设计和执行法律是简单、直接且优先/重要的法律:)。

（我不认为不同意！我也是一个计算机极客，这些东西对我来说也很重要并且可见，但是让我们意识到a）围绕如此复杂且不断变化的事物制定和实施法律的实际复杂性，以及b）世界上绝大多数人的实际优先事项可能是:)

children.author: johnnyanmac
children.textRawHtml:
a) no one said it was easy. I'd just like some progress on it instead of another 30 years of slowly losing rights to things I buy.

b) yes, there are more important regulations to take into account. But governments also deal with hundreds of proposals. I can want proper digital ownership and still care about building more housing, creating stronger labor protections, and overhaulijg urban development.
children.textZhHtml:
a) 没人说这很容易。我只希望在这方面能取得一些进展，而不是再花30年时间慢慢失去对所购物品的权利。

b) 是的，还有更重要的法规需要考虑。但政府也要处理数百项提案。我可以期望拥有正当的数字所有权，同时依然关心建设更多住房、加强劳工保护以及彻底改革城市发展。

children.author: _davide_
children.textRawHtml: A simple law: everything the customer buys must always behave *in favor of the customer over anything else*. If the product/service contradicts this, it must be fully stated before the purchase and cannot be updated. <= This would be a sane balance.
children.textZhHtml: 一条简单的法则：客户购买的所有东西都必须始终*有利于客户*。如果产品/服务与此相矛盾，则必须在购买前充分说明，并且不能更新。 <=这将是一个合理的平衡。

children.author: LordDragonfang
children.textRawHtml:
Depending on how you (you specifically) are defining "fully stated":

1. This is very literally what already happens, it's called a EULA.

2. In practice this means you are required to personally come to the customer's house to fix bugs (or any other ridiculous edge case that wasn't "fully stated"). As much as I strongly agree the law should swing much further in the direction of the consumer, as GP points out, that only holds until it's your obligation to the customer on the line. "In favor of the customer over anything else" is not a legally viable clause.
children.textZhHtml:
这取决于你（你本人）如何定义“完全说明”：

1. 这实际上就是正在发生的事情，它被称为最终用户许可协议。

2. 在实践中，这意味着你需要亲自到客户家里去修复漏洞（或任何其他未被“完全说明”的荒谬边缘情况）。尽管我非常赞同法律应该大幅向消费者倾斜，但正如GP所指出的，这只有在不是你对客户承担义务时才成立。“不惜一切代价偏向客户”在法律上不是一个可行的条款。

children.author: _davide_
children.textRawHtml:
> This is very literally what already happens, it's called a EULA. Yes, but they "reserve the right" to update whenever, making it pointless

> "In favor of the customer over anything else" is not a legally viable clause. I'm sure that legislators could put the principle down in a much clearer way. What's lacking is the will.
children.textZhHtml:
> 这实际上已经发生了，它被称为 EULA。 是的，但他们“保留随时更新的权利”，这使得它毫无意义

>“有利于客户而不是其他任何事情”并不是一个合法可行的条款。 我确信立法者可以以更清晰的方式制定这一原则。缺少的是意志。

children.author: LordDragonfang
children.textRawHtml:
> I'm sure that legislators could put the principle down in a much clearer way.

That's precisely the problem here. You're "sure" that a problem you don't actually fully understand is trivially solved in a simple manner, when the reality is that this sort of thing is incredibly complicated, and there's a multitude of reasons and competing interests that have resulted in the current equilibrium.

This is the sort of change that requires a country's laws to have to be rewritten from the ground-up, because it invalidates so many assumptions. It's the sort of thing you typically need a constitutional amendment (or at least, a novel interpretation of the existing text) for.

So, yeah, they're lacking the political will for that.
children.textZhHtml:
> 我相信立法者可以以更清晰的方式阐述这一原则。

这正是问题所在。你“确信”一个你实际上并不完全理解的问题可以通过简单的方式得到简单的解决，而现实是这种事情非常复杂，并且有多种原因和相互竞争的利益导致了当前的平衡。

这种变化需要一个国家的法律必须从头开始重写，因为它使很多假设失效。这通常需要宪法修正案（或者至少是对现有文本的新颖解释）。

所以，是的，他们缺乏这样做的政治意愿。

children.author: reichstein
children.textRawHtml:
It's not that I don't agree, but lawyers will then ask you to define "buy" in such a way that it is distinguishable from a perpetual lease with a cancellation clause _to buyers_, without also disallowing a lot of actually useful leasing agreements.

The thing is, you never did _buy_ that Steam game. And you never bought the software on the TV, which you did buy the hardware of, you bought a software lease along with the hardware.

The latter case I can see something to do about - define the software and its functionality as an "essential component" of the hardware, and require companies to not break essential components of hardware they sell. They can stop offering online services, but the rest of the device should keep working.

For pure software leases, I don't see a good way to not have them be whatever the contract say they are, not without reclassifying them as something else than a copyrighted work. (But then "sellers" should be very clear what you're "buying".)
children.textZhHtml:
并不是我不同意，而是律师会要求你对“购买”进行定义，以区别于带有取消条款的永久租赁_买家_，同时又不允许签订许多实际有用的租赁协议。

问题是，你从未_购买_过那个 Steam 游戏。 而且你从来没有购买过电视上的软件，你确实购买了电视的硬件，你购买了软件租赁和硬件。

后一种情况我可以看到一些要做的事情 - 将软件及其功能定义为硬件的“基本组件”，并要求公司不要破坏他们销售的硬件的基本组件。他们可以停止提供在线服务，但设备的其余部分应该继续工作。

对于纯软件租赁，我看不出有什么好方法可以不让它们成为合同所说的任何内容，而不是将它们重新分类为受版权保护的作品之外的东西。 （但是“卖家”应该非常清楚你要“买”什么。）

children.author: sirwhinesalot
children.textRawHtml:
A lease should have a clear timetable. How long am I leasing this thing for? 1 year? 3 years?

I'm fine with companies leasing software. I don't like it, and I much prefer buying, but that's fine. That is what software subscriptions are, the terms and conditions are clear.

"Buying" something where access can be revoked at any time, for any reason, needs to become illegal.
children.textZhHtml:
租赁应该有明确的时间表。我租这东西租多久？1年？3年？

我能接受公司租赁软件。我不喜欢这样，我更喜欢购买，但这没关系。软件订阅就是这样，条款和条件都很明确。

“购买”某种随时可能因任何理由被撤销访问权限的东西，这种做法必须被定为非法。

children.author: yason
children.textRawHtml:
> It's not that I don't agree, but lawyers will then ask you to define "buy" in such a way that

One fundamental thing would be to make it illegal to lawyer redefinitions of common words. If the sales of a game uses the word "buy" it should not be legal to redefine "buy" in that context to actually mean lump-sum lease or something.

I'm pretty sure the game studios wouldn't like me to buy their games if I were to amend the terms of sale with a clause in fine print that the term "pay" means "setting up a temporary IOU for which I reserve the right to have it resolved into nothing at my discretion". So, I'll pay later if I decide pay, maybe never. That's effectively what their "buy" means.
children.textZhHtml:
> 并不是我不同意，但律师们会要求你以某种方式定义“购买”

一个根本的解决办法是，将律师对常用词汇的重新定义定为非法。如果游戏销售中使用了“购买”一词，就不应允许在该语境下将“购买”重新定义为实际上指一次性租赁或其他什么。

我很确定，如果我在销售条款中用一行小字添加一个条款，规定“支付”一词的意思是“设立一个临时借据，我保留自行决定将其化为乌有的权利”，那么游戏工作室就不会喜欢我购买他们的游戏了。也就是说，等我决定支付时我再付，也许永远不付。这实际上就是他们所谓的“购买”的意思。

children.author: short_sells_poo
children.textRawHtml:
> it should not be legal to redefine "buy"

Your post made me go on a thought exercise: imagine a world where you could either own or lease software products. Game developers could decide whether their customers can actually buy the product (which involves ownership) or just the traditional licensing/leasing, which is the prevailing model today.

How would ownership actually work? What does ownership of digital goods mean? Can they even be owned? You get 1 copy of the bytes? How is this enforced? What's to stop you from just selling it multiple times?

If you can't resell it, then what do you actually own? The right to use the product in perpetuity? But that's just another form of lease, no?

Am I getting bogged down in semantics?
children.textZhHtml:
> 重新定义“购买”不应该是合法的

你的帖子让我进行了一次思考练习：想象一个你可以拥有或租赁软件产品的世界。游戏开发商可以决定他们的客户是否可以实际购买产品（涉及所有权），或者只是传统的许可/租赁（这是当今流行的模式）。

所有权实际上如何运作？数字商品的所有权意味着什么？它们甚至可以被拥有吗？您获得了 1 个字节的副本吗？这是如何执行的？是什么阻止您多次出售它？

如果您无法转售它，那么您实际上拥有什么？永久使用该产品的权利？但这只是另一种形式的租赁，不是吗？

我是否陷入了语义的困境？

stories.title: OpenWrt One – Open Hardware Router
stories.titleZh: OpenWrt One – 开源硬件路由器

comments.author: PaulKeeble
comments.textRawHtml:
They are working on an OpenWRT Two at the moment which will be Wifi 7.

children.author: vsviridov
children.textRawHtml: Why do they always have to look like some unholy blend of a cybernetic spider and a Knight Rider? What happened to a plain unassuming looking piece of industrial hardware...
children.textZhHtml: 为什么他们总是看起来像控制论蜘蛛和骑士的邪恶混合体？一个看起来不起眼的工业硬件发生了什么......

children.author: Karliss
children.textRawHtml:
Wifi 5-7 happened, now operating at 3 different frequency ranges (2.4, 5 and 6Ghz) and using techniques like beam forming and MIMO. All those antennas need to go somewhere.

If you want plain unassuming looking hardware get dedicated wifi access points and place them all over the building. There are plenty of those shaped liked big smoke detectors.

If you want single device there are also quite a few trash can shaped home routers.
children.textZhHtml:
Wifi 5-7 已经出现，现在在 3 个不同的频率范围（2.4、5 和 6Ghz）下运行，并使用波束成形和 MIMO 等技术。所有这些天线都需要安装到某个地方。

如果您想要外观朴素、不起眼的硬件，请获取专用的 WiFi 接入点，并将它们放置在整个建筑物中。有很多类似大型烟雾探测器的形状。

如果您想要单个设备，还有很多垃圾桶形状的家用路由器。

children.author: buildsjets
children.textRawHtml: Why does Wifi 5-7 require designing the case so it is shaped like an F-117 fighter jet instead of a box of candy?
children.textZhHtml: 为什么 Wifi 5-7 需要将外壳设计成 F-117 战斗机的形状，而不是一盒糖果？

children.author: Karliss
children.textRawHtml:
Don't buy one which looks like that if visuals are important for you. I already told that there are plenty of models which avoids this design and form factor. Do I have to spell out specific manufacturers and models?

Almost none of the Ubiquity stuff looks like that. Xiaomi has plenty of white/gray cylinders or boxes with rounded corners. TP-link has whole Deco series, Asus has ZenWifi series. Majority of MikroTik non rack mounted hardware also targets more neutral design.

You also have to consider who is the target audience for dedicated all in one wifi routers. Majority of regular people are fine with the WiFi that's builtin the modem provided by their ISP. Any serious commercial office will have the IT team to setup separate (rack mounted) router/switches and ceiling mounted access points that look like previously mentioned smoke alarms. People with large enough house to need multiple access points but aren't IT specialists willing to wire up Ethernet everywhere -> various product lines described as mesh routers. Like the trash can shaped TP link Deco series and similar from other manufacturers. If your house is not that big, nothing stops you to buy one of them and ignore the mesh functionality. That leaves people living in small enough house/apartment to be served by single router/switch/Wifi access point combo but for some reason not being satisfied what the ISP provides and also wanting multiple wired connections. Exclude the IT specialists willing to set up home lab and you are left with gamers (potentially impressed by black spider) and few others who have hopefully have enough rationality to place the router where it's not an eyesore or picking some of the previously mentioned stuff.

Another factor is move from antennas that are simple correctly size wire maybe with some spiral which easily fits in small rounded antenna to flat pcb antennas which encourage more rectangular design of the antenna housing and rest of the router. A lot of it is still partially just for the show, trying to give the impression "this one has more/bigger antennas must be better WiFi", but oversized partially empty plastic antenna housing were a thing even before current spider trend.

White slightly rounded 8 legged spider still looks like spider. Trash cans have a bunch of antennas but they hide them in larger volume. Dedicated access points have the advantage of being placed more predictably (near ceiling with little obstacles), they also have advantage of being distributed less work for each of them instead of single router covering whole house.
children.textZhHtml:
如果视觉效果对您来说很重要，请不要购买看起来像这样的产品。我已经说过，有很多型号可以避免这种设计和外形尺寸。我必须说出具体的制造商和型号吗？

几乎所有 Ubiquity 的东西都不是这样的。小米有很多白色/灰色的圆柱体或圆角盒子。 TP-link有整个Deco系列，华硕有ZenWifi系列。大多数 MikroTik 非机架安装硬件也以更中性的设计为目标。

您还必须考虑谁是专用全合一 WiFi 路由器的目标受众。 大多数普通人都可以使用 ISP 提供的内置调制解调器的 WiFi。 任何严肃的商业办公室都会让 IT 团队设置独立的（机架安装式）路由器/交换机和吸顶式接入点，类似于前面提到的烟雾报警器。 人们拥有足够大的房子，需要多个接入点，但 IT 专家不愿意在任何地方连接以太网 -> 被称为网状路由器的各种产品线。就像垃圾桶形状的 TP link Deco 系列和其他制造商的类似产品。如果您的房子不是那么大，没有什么可以阻止您购买其中之一并忽略网格功能。 这使得居住在足够小的房屋/公寓中的人们可以通过单个路由器/交换机/Wifi 接入点组合提供服务，但由于某种原因对 ISP 提供的服务不满意，并且还需要多个有线连接。排除愿意建立家庭实验室的IT专家，剩下的就是游戏玩家（可能会被黑蜘蛛打动）和少数其他人，他们希望有足够的理性将路由器放置在不碍眼的地方，或者选择前面提到的一些东西。

另一个因素是从简单的、正确尺寸的电线（可能带有一些螺旋线，可以轻松安装在小型圆形天线中）转向扁平的PCB天线，这鼓励天线外壳和路由器其余部分的更多矩形设计。其中很多仍然只是为了展示，试图给人一种印象“这个有更多/更大的天线一定是更好的 WiFi”，但超大的部分空塑料天线外壳甚至在当前蜘蛛趋势之前就已经是一种现象了。

白色略圆的 8 腿蜘蛛仍然看起来像蜘蛛。垃圾桶有一堆天线，但它们隐藏的体积更大。专用接入点的优点是放置位置更加可预测（靠近天花板，几乎没有障碍物），它们还具有为每个接入点分配较少工作的优点，而不是覆盖整个房子的单个路由器。

I have a U7 Lite and it is very directional compared to other routers I have used (spider style, trash can style, etc.)

可能是我疯了，但所有外置天线难道不是能让信号方向性更具灵活性吗？

children.author: bcrl
children.textRawHtml:
The U7 Lite only does 2x2 MIMO. Compared to 4x4 MIMO in the U6 LR, the U7 Lite therefore does a much poorer job at beamforming (directing the energy of the signal towards the device).

Personally, I find it better to have multiple low end access points (like the TP-Link Archer C80 which has 3x3 MIMO on 5 GHz) deployed to achieve excellent coverage in a house. Sadly, the U7 line is a bit too expensive for that. Plus, I'm loathe to deal with UniFi deployments now that I am well versed in the glass jaws in the platform.

There really is space in the market for a product line that is basically what UniFi is, but done "right". Ie: can be debugged or you can fix it without an internet connection or recover the system when the owner forgets the password and lost access to the email account used for 2FA. UniFi is an absolute nightmare the moment anything goes even slightly wrong.
children.textZhHtml:
U7 Lite仅支持2x2 MIMO。与U6 LR的4x4 MIMO相比，U7 Lite在波束成形（将信号能量定向传输至设备）方面的表现要差得多。

就我个人而言，我觉得部署多个低端接入点（比如支持5GHz频段3x3 MIMO的TP-Link Archer C80）能更好地实现全屋出色覆盖。遗憾的是，U7系列的价格对此来说有点太高了。而且，在彻底了解了该平台存在的致命弱点后，我非常不愿意再折腾UniFi的部署了。

市场上确实存在一个空白，需要一条产品线，它基本上就是UniFi的样子，但能“做对”。也就是说：可以进行调试，或者在没有网络连接的情况下进行修复，或者在机主忘记密码且无法访问用于双重认证的邮箱时恢复系统。UniFi只要出一点点小问题，简直就是场彻头彻尾的噩梦。

children.author: diegs
children.textRawHtml:
I went from Unifi to Ruckus APs and never looked back.

You can find older generations on eBay very inexpensively for what they are, install Ruckus Unleashed firmware on them, and operate them completely locally.

They have the best beamforming antennas in the industry and their firmware is rock solid. I'm on some very old models (2.4ghz and 5ghz only) but they work fantastically well. I'll probably upgrade to some wifi 6 models soon.

But for my uses 500-600mbps rock solid throughout my house is plenty. Anything where I want max throughput/low latency/low jitter (mainly gaming) is hardwired. For wifi I care much more about complete and consistent coverage.
children.textZhHtml:
我从Unifi换成了Ruckus AP，从此再没回头。

你可以在eBay上以极低的价格淘到上一代产品，刷入Ruckus Unleashed固件，就能完全在本地运行。

它们拥有业界顶级的波束成形天线，固件也极其稳定。我现在用的还是些很老的型号（仅支持2.4GHz和5GHz），但表现依然非常出色。我可能很快就会升级到Wi-Fi 6的型号。

但就我的使用需求而言，全屋500-600Mbps的稳定网速已经绰绰有余。凡是需要极限吞吐量、低延迟和低抖动（主要是打游戏）的场景，我都直接插网线。对于Wi-Fi，我更看重的是全面且稳定的覆盖。

children.author: zhengyi13
children.textRawHtml: Yes, perhaps they do allow for greater flexibility, but that's complex and difficult to do well/reliably, and doing it well/reliably requires signal analysis gear and software modeling that's out of the reach of normal consumers.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，也许它们确实能提供更大的灵活性，但这很复杂，很难做好或做到可靠，而要做好或做到可靠，需要普通消费者无法企及的信号分析设备和软件建模。

As for why it needs multiple antennas, it's for MIMO and beam forming.

至于为什么需要多个天线，是为了 MIMO 和波束成形。
